228	- **MCP tool descriptions are LLM-facing, not REST-API-facing.** Write them for natural-language selection; test against the 4 canonical NL queries in the roadmap.
229	
230	---
231	
232	## 11. What NOT to do
233	
234	- ❌ Don't add ADF or Airflow as a queryable datasource in the connection editor / check editor / suite model.
235	- ❌ Don't bypass the `OrchestrationProvider` abstraction with provider-specific branching in service code.
236	- ❌ Don't deepen Azure lock-in: no reading Entra/OIDC provider claims in route/service code (depend on the generic `get_current_user`), no hardcoded Azure resource names/endpoints in business logic, no Azure-only assumptions baked into container images. Azure is one impl behind each seam — see ADR [0010](docs/adr/0010-provider-agnostic-infrastructure-seams.md) / [0013](docs/adr/0013-marketplace-distribution-and-anti-lock-in.md).
237	- ❌ Don't `git commit --no-verify` past hooks. If a hook fails, fix the underlying issue.
238	- ❌ Don't commit `.env` files. Use `.env.example` / `.env.app.example` as the templates.
239	- ❌ Don't put a credential — **even a local/mock one** — in any git-tracked file (templates, `scripts/`, CI, compose). Env templates ship the secret keys **blank** with the shape in a comment; `scripts/setup.sh` generates the local-dev password into the gitignored `.env`/`.env.app` on first run. Non-secret config defaults and non-secret identifiers (db/user name) may stay populated.
240	- ❌ Don't drop columns in the same PR as the code change that stops using them. Two-step it.
241	- ❌ Don't fix bugs silently. Raise a GitHub issue, then PR with `Fixes #N`.
242	- ❌ Don't batch unrelated changes into one commit. One functionality per commit.
243	- ❌ Don't track GX Core at "latest." Pin the version in `environment.yml` — GX v1 API has drifted across point releases.
244	- ❌ Don't use venv or poetry for backend dev. Conda only.
245	- ❌ Don't write the MCP layer before Week 7. The service layer must stabilise first.

- **MCP tool descriptions are LLM-facing, not REST-API-facing.** Write them for natural-language selection; test against the 4 canonical NL queries in the roadmap.

---

## 11. What NOT to do

- ❌ Don't add ADF or Airflow as a queryable datasource in the connection editor / check editor / suite model.
- ❌ Don't bypass the `OrchestrationProvider` abstraction with provider-specific branching in service code.
- ❌ Don't deepen Azure lock-in: no reading Entra/OIDC provider claims in route/service code (depend on the generic `get_current_user`), no hardcoded Azure resource names/endpoints in business logic, no Azure-only assumptions baked into container images. Azure is one impl behind each seam — see ADR [0010](docs/adr/0010-provider-agnostic-infrastructure-seams.md) / [0013](docs/adr/0013-marketplace-distribution-and-anti-lock-in.md).
- ❌ Don't `git commit --no-verify` past hooks. If a hook fails, fix the underlying issue.
- ❌ Don't commit `.env` files. Use `.env.example` / `.env.app.example` as the templates.
- ❌ Don't put a credential — **even a local/mock one** — in any git-tracked file (templates, `scripts/`, CI, compose). Env templates ship the secret keys **blank** with the shape in a comment; `scripts/setup.sh` generates the local-dev password into the gitignored `.env`/`.env.app` on first run. Non-secret config defaults and non-secret identifiers (db/user name) may stay populated.
- ❌ Don't drop columns in the same PR as the code change that stops using them. Two-step it.
- ❌ Don't fix bugs silently. Raise a GitHub issue, then PR with `Fixes #N`.
- ❌ Don't batch unrelated changes into one commit. One functionality per commit.
- ❌ Don't track GX Core at "latest." Pin the version in `environment.yml` — GX v1 API has drifted across point releases.
- ❌ Don't use venv or poetry for backend dev. Conda only.
- ❌ Don't write the MCP layer before Week 7. The service layer must stabilise first.

input.new_string:
**Decision & legal**
- [ ] Amend/append ADR recording the free-OSS + BYOL distribution decision (supersedes ADR 0013 §5's entitlement/license-key line; keep MIT) — **in progress: ADR 0031, PR #733**
- [ ] THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES (or SPDX SBOM) generated and shipped in images + GitHub releases; wire generation into CI/publish
- [ ] **G-h:** paid Databricks workspace before any commercial demo/use (Free Edition is non-commercial-only; decision of 2026-07-03 stands — trigger is exactly a listing)
- [ ] Marketplace seller registration + per-marketplace review (Partner Center for Azure Phase 1); note free offers still go through certification
- [ ] **Listing terms / EULA:** attach license terms in Partner Center — required even for $0 offers (Standard Contract or MIT-based custom terms). *(ADR 0013 §5 survivor — a free offer does not waive this.)*
- [ ] **SOC 2 / pen test posture decided:** enterprise-procurement ask independent of price (ADR 0013 §5 survivor). Decide defer-until-demand vs pre-emptive, and record the decision — don't leave it unowned.
- [ ] **DPA:** *not needed* while there is no hosted/paid service — self-hosted BYOL creates no data-processor relationship. Recorded here as a conscious disposition (ADR 0013 §5 survivor); revisit the day a hosted tier exists.
- [ ] **Tax/banking (Partner Center payout profile):** *not needed* for a free offer — recorded as a conscious disposition (ADR 0013 §5 survivor); required the day any paid offer/tier ships (ADR 0031 Decision 5 keeps that door open).
- [ ] Support expectations stated: SUPPORT.md (community support channels for free OSS; SLA only if a paid tier ever appears)
- [ ] Privacy posture stated: a short statement that DataQ is self-hosted and phones nothing home (verifiably true today — keep it that way)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
Documentation/tracking record of the 2026-07-09 marketplace-listing readiness review, updated for the **distribution model chosen 2026-07-09: free open-source software + customer-deployed BYOL** (refines ADR 0013 — the "licensing model + entitlement/license-key" line in §5 is superseded by this choice; no license-key/entitlement machinery is needed when the license is the OSS grant itself).

## License audit result (2026-07-09): free OSS + BYOL is CLEAR — no contradictions

- **Project license:** MIT (`LICENSE`, © 2026 Arijit Roy) — ideal for free OSS + BYOL; marketplaces support free/BYOL container offers of OSS.
- **Backend (full conda env, 239 packages):** **zero strong copyleft / source-available licenses.** Key deps confirmed from installed metadata: great-expectations 1.17.2 (Apache-2.0), fastmcp (Apache-2.0), fastapi/sqlalchemy/pydantic/alembic (MIT), celery/uvicorn/pandas/numpy (BSD), snowflake + databricks connectors, boto3, pyiceberg, pyarrow, opentelemetry (Apache-2.0), `caio` (Apache-2.0, verified via bundled COPYING). Weak copyleft only: `psycopg2-binary` (LGPL-3.0, dynamically linked — fine to ship, include notice), `certifi`/`pathspec` (MPL-2.0, file-level).
- **Frontend (node_modules sweep, 643 packages):** **zero strong copyleft.** MIT 526 / Apache-2.0 42 / ISC 33 / BSD 18. Weak copyleft: `lightningcss` (MPL-2.0, build-time only — not distributed), `dompurify` (dual MPL-2.0 OR Apache-2.0 — take Apache). `@fontsource/*` fonts are OFL-1.1 (standard for bundling web fonts). The UNKNOWN-license hits are nested test fixtures inside deps, not in the lockfile — not shipped.
- **Net obligations when distributing images/bundles:** preserve upstream copyright/license notices (MIT/BSD) and Apache-2.0 NOTICE content → ship a THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES file (or SBOM with license data) in images and releases. That's the whole compliance surface.

## Prerequisites (blocking a listing)

**Decision & legal**
- [ ] Amend/append ADR recording the free-OSS + BYOL distribution decision (supersedes ADR 0013 §5's entitlement/license-key line; keep MIT)
- [ ] THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES (or SPDX SBOM) generated and shipped in images + GitHub releases; wire generation into CI/publish
- [ ] **G-h:** paid Databricks workspace before any commercial demo/use (Free Edition is non-commercial-only; decision of 2026-07-03 stands — trigger is exactly a listing)
- [ ] Marketplace seller registration + per-marketplace review (Partner Center for Azure Phase 1); note free offers still go through certification
- [ ] Support expectations stated: SUPPORT.md (community support channels for free OSS; SLA only if a paid tier ever appears)
- [ ] Privacy posture stated: a short statement that DataQ is self-hosted and phones nothing home (verifiably true today — keep it that way)

**Product hygiene (G-i teardown)**
- [ ] G-i deployed-side teardown before any distributable artifact: strip Flows A/B/C, the 5 harness connections, demo suites/users, seeded-breach check from the reference deployment (checklist already in deploy/README.md)
- [ ] #730 — repo-side G-i slice: remove the real prod FQDN from tracked files (backend test, deploy/README, CLAUDE.md)
- [ ] #729 — fix stale SECURITY.md "no versioned releases" claim
- [ ] Split eval vs install paths: `docker-compose.ghcr.yml` always seeds demo data and forces auth-bypass — fine for eval, but a clearly separated non-seeded, auth-enabled install path must exist

**Portability implementations (ADR 0013 Phase 2, still open)**
- [ ] Generic OIDC/JWKS backend token validation behind `get_current_user` (today: `fastapi-azure-auth` only; frontend is already generic per ADR 0028 — backend is the gap)
- [ ] One production-grade non-Azure `SecretStore` (Vault / AWS SM / GCP SM — additive class per ADR 0010)
- [ ] Portable install artifact — Helm chart (ADR 0013's decided shape); the in-repo Terraform is harness-coupled (ADR 0024) and not customer-reusable
- [ ] **G-b** scale-aware execution (#595) — flat-file + UC runners load whole tables into worker pandas; roadmap's own words: "blocks any serious deployment"
- [ ] #725 — rate limiting on the public surface

## Good-to-haves (improve the offer, don't block)

- [ ] README landing polish: screenshots/GIF (currently zero images), customer-voiced pitch above the dev-log status line
- [ ] Prod-ish docker-compose profile (TLS, real auth, non-plaintext secrets) — overlaps the local-first posture work (#591); doubles as the on-prem story
- [ ] Customer-facing upgrade guide (migration-gated deploys exist; the doc doesn't)
- [ ] In-app admin management (G-e: `WORKSPACE_ADMIN_EMAILS` env allowlist is "fine internally, not sellable"); ADR 0026 phase-2 service accounts adjacent
- [ ] Data-access audit trail (G-d/#431) — deploy/README already calls it a prerequisite for regulated (PHI) deployments
- [ ] CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md (standard for an OSS project taking contributions)
- [ ] AWS/GCP IaC (#505) → Phase-3 AWS/GCP listings
- [ ] Architecture-review portability nits: #728 (MCP host allowlist), KV purge-protection default

## Sequencing note

The decision + notices + G-i/#729/#730 hygiene items are cheap — do first. The expensive items (generic backend OIDC, non-Azure SecretStore, Helm chart, #595) are exactly ADR 0013 Phase 2 and mostly already scheduled in v1.1 W4–6; the listing slots naturally after them.
